But looking at it more carefully, `gcc -O3 -o /app/a.out /app/gpt2.c -lm 2>&1 | grep error` - if grep doesn't find "error", it returns non-zero and the && chain stops. So "OK" wasn't printed because grep didn't find "error" (i.e., compilation succeeded without errors). Wait, that's wrong - if grep doesn't find "error", it returns 1, and && would stop. So actually compilation succeeded (no errors found by grep), but the && chain stopped because grep returned non-zero.

Let me fix the command and

rerun.

Actually wait - re-reading: `gcc 2>&1 | grep error && echo 'OK'` - if there are no errors, grep returns 1 (not found), so `echo 'OK'` doesn't execute. So compilation was clean. But then the `&& /app/a.out ...` also wouldn't execute.

So the output stopped because there were NO compilation errors. I need to use `|| echo 'OK'` or just not use grep in the chain.

Let me just compile and run directly.
